clusters.title: Avoid shelling out with unvalidated log arguments

member_findings.title: Avoid shelling out with unvalidated log arguments
member_findings.impact: The new command builds `tail -n {args.lines} {log_path}` from CLI-controlled `--lines` and `--worker_address` and runs it with `shell=True`. A local user who can invoke `dscli logs` can include shell metacharacters in either argument and execute arbitrary commands with the dscli user's privileges before or instead of reading the intended log.
member_findings.fix: Validate `worker_address` as a host:port value and parse `--lines` as a positive integer, then call tail without a shell, for example `subprocess.run(["tail", "-n", str(lines), log_path], ...)`; alternatively implement the tail in Python after resolving and constraining the log path under the log directory.
